Tomato___Early_blight.treatment: Remove infected leaves; apply fungicide every 7-10 days; avoid overhead irrigation.
Tomato___Early_blight.weather: Warm (24-29°C), humid conditions with wet foliage.

Tomato___Late_blight.treatment: Destroy infected plants; apply systemic fungicide; improve field drainage.
Tomato___Late_blight.weather: Cool (10-20°C), wet and foggy weather accelerates spread.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Use certified disease-free seed; spray copper-based bactericide at 10-day intervals.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.weather: Hot (25-30°C), rainy with high humidity promotes infection.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.treatment: Improve greenhouse ventilation; apply protectant fungicide at first sign.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.treatment: Remove infected lower leaves; avoid wetting foliage; apply fungicide.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.weather: Warm (20-25°C), wet weather with frequent rain or dew.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.treatment: Apply miticide; increase humidity; introduce predatory mites.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.weather: Hot (>27°C), dry, dusty conditions favour mite outbreaks.

Tomato___Target_Spot.treatment: Improve air circulation; apply fungicide at first symptoms.
Tomato___Target_Spot.weather: Warm (25-30°C) with high humidity and leaf wetness.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.pathogen: Tomato yellow leaf curl virus (TYLCV) via whitefly
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.treatment: Remove infected plants; use reflective mulches; apply insecticide to control whitefly.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.weather: Warm dry conditions favour whitefly populations and virus spread.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.pesticide: No direct pesticide; control aphid vectors with Imidacloprid
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.treatment: Remove and destroy infected plants; disinfect tools; use resistant varieties.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.weather: Virus spreads mechanically; high temperatures weaken plant resistance.

Tomato___healthy.treatment: Continue regular monitoring and good agricultural practices.
Tomato___healthy.weather: Maintain optimal temperature (21-27°C) and adequate moisture.

Potato___Early_blight.treatment: Rotate crops every 3 years; apply fungicide at first symptoms.
Potato___Early_blight.weather: Warm days (20-30°C) with high humidity trigger outbreaks.

Potato___Late_blight.treatment: Haulm destruction before harvest; use resistant varieties; foliar fungicide spray.
Potato___Late_blight.weather: Wet, cool spells (10-15°C) are ideal for disease spread.

Potato___healthy.treatment: Continue normal irrigation and balanced fertilisation.
Potato___healthy.weather: Keep soil evenly moist; avoid waterlogging.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.treatment: Plant resistant hybrids; apply foliar fungicide at early rust appearance.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.weather: Cool (16-23°C), humid with morning dew favours spore germination.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.treatment: Use resistant hybrids; apply fungicide at VT/R1 stage.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.weather: Moderate temps (18-27°C) with extended wet, humid conditions.

Corn_(maize)___healthy.weather: Optimal growth at 24-30°C with regular moisture.

Apple___Apple_scab.treatment: Prune infected branches; destroy fallen leaves; apply protective fungicide at bud break.
Apple___Apple_scab.weather: Cool, moist spring (18-24°C) with frequent rain.

Apple___Black_rot.treatment: Prune and burn infected branches; apply fungicide from pink bud stage.
Apple___Black_rot.weather: Warm (25-30°C), wet periods during growing season.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.treatment: Remove nearby cedar/juniper hosts; apply protective fungicide at bud break.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.weather: Spring rains (18-24°C) trigger spore release from cedars.

Apple___healthy.weather: Ideal growth at 18-24°C with moderate rainfall.

Grape___Black_rot.treatment: Remove mummified fruit; prune for airflow; apply fungicide pre/post-bloom.
Grape___Black_rot.weather: Warm (26-30°C), wet weather during bloom is most critical.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).pesticide: No curative fungicide; trunk injection with Thiabendazole may help
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).treatment: Prune during dry conditions; remove and burn affected wood; protect pruning wounds.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).weather: Warm, dry summers after wet winters favour disease expression.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.treatment: Remove and destroy infected leaves; apply protective fungicide early season.

Grape___healthy.treatment: Standard vineyard monitoring and canopy management.
Grape___healthy.weather: Optimal growth at 15-25°C with moderate humidity.
